reasoning: WTI crude is heavily influenced by random walk dynamics, but faces slight structural headwinds entering 2026. Forecasters like J.P. Morgan and Goldman Sachs have a structurally bearish outlook for 2026 due to OPEC+ production cuts unwinding, forecasting prices dropping into the low to mid $50s (compared to current spot ~56.80). Recent short-term price action has also been downward over the past month, despite a minor end-of-week bounce. We start slightly bearish in the short term, and as time horizons extend to 1 month and 3 months, uncertainty increases, pushing the probabilities back toward 0.50.
